author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

title: John 1.11

source.title: NPNF1-14. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of St. John and the Epistle to the Hebrews

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He came unto His own, and His own received Him not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Beloved, God being loving towards man and beneficent, does and contrives all things in order that we may shine in virtue, and as desiring that we be well approved by Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And to this end He draws no one by force or compulsion: but by persuasion and benefits He draws all that will, and wins them to Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore when He came, some received Him, and others received Him not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He will have no unwilling, no forced domestic, but all of their own will and choice, and grateful to Him for their service.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Men, as needing the ministry of servants, keep many in that state even against their will, by the law of ownership; but God, being without wants, and not standing in need of anything of ours, but doing all only for our salvation makes us absolute in this matter, and therefore lays neither force nor compulsion on any of those who are unwilling.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He looks only to our advantage: and to be drawn unwilling to a service like this is the same as not serving at all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Why then,” says one, “does He punish those who will not listen to Him, and why hath He threatened hell to those who endure not His commands?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because, being Good exceedingly, He cares even for those who obey Him not, and withdraws not from them who start back and flee from Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But when we had rejected the first way of His beneficence, and had refused to come by the path of persuasion and kind treatment, then He brought in upon us the other way, that of correction and punishments; most bitter indeed, but still necessary, when the former is disregarded.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now lawgivers also appoint many and grievous penalties against offenders, and yet we feel no aversion to them for this; we even honor them the more on account of the punishments they have enacted, and because though not needing a single thing that we have, and often not knowing who they should be that should enjoy the help afforded by their written laws, they still took care for the good ordering of our lives, rewarding those who live virtuously, and checking by punishments the intemperate, and those who would mar the repose of others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if we admire and love these men, ought we not much more to marvel at and love God on account of His so great care?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the difference between their and His forethought regarding us is infinite.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Unspeakable of a truth are the riches of the goodness of God, and passing all excess.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Consider; “He came to His own,” not for His personal need, (for, as I said, the Divinity is without wants,) but to do good unto His own people.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet not even so did His own receive Him, when He came to His own for their advantage, but repelled Him, and not this only, but they even cast Him out of the vineyard, and slew Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet not for this even did He shut them out from repentance, but granted them, if they had been willing, after such wickedness as this, to wash off all their transgressions by faith in Him, and to be made equal to those who had done no such thing, but are His especial friends.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And that I say not this at random, or for persuasion’s sake, all the history of the blessed Paul loudly declares.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when he, who after the Cross persecuted Christ, and had stoned His martyr Stephen by those many hands, repented, and condemned his former sins, and ran to Him whom he had persecuted, He immediately enrolled him among His friends, and the chiefest of them, having appointed him a herald and teacher of all the world, who had been “a blasphemer, and persecutor, and injurious.” ( 1 Tim. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Even as he rejoicing at the lovingkindness of God, has proclaimed aloud, and has not been ashamed, but having recorded in his writings, as on a pillar, the deeds formerly dared by him, has exhibited them to all; thinking it better that his former life should be placarded in sight of all, so that the greatness of the free gift of God might appear, than that he should obscure His ineffable and indescribable lovingkindness by hesitating to parade before all men his own error.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore continually he treats of his persecution, his plottings, his wars against the Church, at one time saying, “I am not meet to be called an Apostle, because I persecuted the Church of God” ( 1 Cor. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 9 ); at another, “Jesus came into the world to save sinners, of whom I am chief.” ( 1 Tim. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And again, “Ye have heard of my conversation in time past in the Jews’ religion, how that beyond measure I persecuted the church of God, and wasted it.” ( Gal. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For making as it were a kind of return to Christ for His longsuffering towards him, by showing who it was, what a hater and enemy that He saved, he declared with much openness the warfare which at the first with all zeal he warred against Christ; and with this he holds forth good hopes to those who despaired of their condition.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he says, that Christ accepted him, in order that in him first He “might show forth all longsuffering” ( 1 Tim. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 16 ), and the abundant riches of His goodness, “for a pattern to them that should hereafter believe in Him to life everlasting.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because the things which they had dared were too great for any pardon which the Evangelist declaring, said,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He came to His own, and His own received Him not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Whence came He, who filleth all things, and who is everywhere present?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What place did He empty of His presence, who holdeth and graspeth all things in His hand?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He exchanged not one place for another; how should He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But by His coming down to us He effected this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since, though being in the world, He did not seem to be there, because He was not yet known, but afterwards manifested Himself by deigning to take upon Him our flesh, he (St. John) calls this manifestation and descent “a coming.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: One might wonder at the disciple who is not ashamed of the dishonor of his Teacher, but even records the insolence which was used towards Him: yet this is no small proof of his truth-loving disposition.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And besides, he who feels shame should feel it for those who have offered an insult, not for the person outraged.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Indeed He by this very thing shone the brighter, as taking, even after the insult, so much care for those who had offered it; while they appeared ungrateful and accursed in the eyes of all men, for having rejected Him who came to bring them so great goods, as hateful to them, and an enemy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And not only in this were they hurt, but also in not obtaining what they obtained who received Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What did these obtain?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “As many as received Him, to them gave He power to become the sons of God,” says the Evangelist.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Why then, O blessed one, dost thou not also tell us the punishment of them who received Him not?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast said that they were ‘His own,’ and that when ‘He came to His own, they received Him not’; but what they shall suffer for this, what punishment they shall undergo, thou hast not gone on to add.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet so thou wouldest the more have terrified them, and have softened the hardness of their insanity by threatening.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore then hast thou been silent?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And what other punishment,” he would say, “can be greater than this, that when power is offered them to become sons of God, they do not become so, but willingly deprive themselves of such nobility and honor as this?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Although their punishment shall not even stop at this point, that they gain no good, but moreover the unquenchable fire shall receive them, as in going on he has more plainly revealed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But for the present he speaks of the unutterable goods of those who received Him, and sets these words in brief before us, saying, “As many as received Him, to them gave He power to become sons of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Whether bond or free, whether Greeks or barbarians or Scythians, unlearned or learned, female or male, children or old men, in honor or dishonor, rich or poor, rulers or private persons, all, He saith, are deemed worthy the same privilege; for faith and the grace of the Spirit, removing the inequality caused by worldly things, hath moulded all to one fashion, and stamped them with one impress, the King’s.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What can equal this lovingkindness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: A king, who is framed of the same clay with us, does not deign to enrol among the royal host his fellow-servants, who share the same nature with himself, and in character often are better than he, if they chance to be slaves; but the Only-Begotten Son of God did not disdain to reckon among the company of His children both publicans, sorcerers, and slaves, nay, men of less repute and greater poverty than these, maimed in body, and suffering from ten thousand ills.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Such is the power of faith in Him, such the excess of His grace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And as the element of fire, when it meets with ore from the mine, straightway of earth makes it gold, even so and much more Baptism makes those who are washed to be of gold instead of clay; the Spirit at that time falling like fire into our souls, burning up the “image of the earthy” ( 1 Cor. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 49 ), and producing “the image of the heavenly,” fresh coined, bright and glittering, as from the furnace-mould.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why then did he say not that “He made them sons of God,” but that “He gave them power to become sons of God”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To show that we need much zeal to keep the image of sonship impressed on us at Baptism, all through without spot or soil ; and at the same time to show that no one shall be able to take this power from us, unless we are the first to deprive ourselves of it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if among men, those who have received the absolute control of any matters have well-nigh as much power as those who gave them the charge; much more shall we, who have obtained such honor from God, be, if we do noth ing unworthy of this power, stronger than all; because He who put this honor in our hands is greater and better than all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At the same time too he wishes to show, that not even does grace come upon man irrespectively, but upon those who desire and take pains for it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it lies in the power of these to become (His) children since if they do not themselves first make the choice, the gift does not come upon them, nor have any effect.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Having therefore everywhere excluded compulsion and pointing to (man’s) voluntary choice and free power, he has said the same now.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For even in these mystical blessings, it is, on the one hand, God’s part, to give the grace, on the other, man’s to supply faith; and in after time there needs for what remains much earnestness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In order to preserve our purity, it is not sufficient for us merely to have been baptized and to have believed, but we must if we will continually enjoy this brightness, display a life worthy of it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This then is God’s work in us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To have been born the mystical Birth, and to have been cleansed from all our former sins, comes from Baptism; but to remain for the future pure, never again after this to admit any stain belongs to our own power and diligence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this is the reason why he reminds us of the manner of the birth, and by comparison with fleshly pangs shows its excellence, when he says,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Who were born, not of blood, nor of the will of the flesh, but of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This he has done, in order that, considering the vileness, and lowness of the first birth, which is “of blood,” and “the will of the flesh,” and perceiving the highness and nobleness of the second, which is by grace, we may form from thence some great opinion of it, and one worthy of the gift of Him who hath begotten us, and for the future exhibit much earnestness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For there is no small fear, lest, having sometime defiled that beautiful robe by our after sloth and transgressions, we be cast out from the inner room and bridal chamber, like the five foolish virgins, or him who had not on a wedding garment. ( Matt. xxv.; Matt. xxii.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He too was one of the guests, for he had been invited; but because, after the invitation and so great an honor, he behaved with insolence towards Him who had invited him, hear what punishment he suffers, how pitiable, fit subject for many tears.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when he comes to partake of that splendid table, not only is he forbidden the least, but bound hand and foot alike, is carried into outer darkness, to undergo eternal and endless wailing and gnashing of teeth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, beloved, let not us either expect that faith is sufficient to us for salvation; for if we do not show forth a pure life, but come clothed with garments unworthy of this blessed calling, nothing hinders us from suffering the same as that wretched one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is strange that He, who is God and King, is not ashamed of men who are vile, beggars, and of no repute, but brings even them of the cross ways to that table; while we manifest so much insensibility, as not even to be made better by so great an honor, but even after the call remain in our old wickedness, insolently abusing the unspeakable lovingkindness of Him who hath called us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it was not for this that He called us to the spiritual and awful communion of His mysteries, that we should enter with our former wickedness; but that, putting off our filthiness, we should change our raiment to such as becomes those who are entertained in palaces.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if we will not act worthily of that calling this no longer rests with Him who hath honored us, but with ourselves; it is not He that casts us out from that admirable company of guests, but we cast out ourselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He has done all His part.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He has made the marriage, He has provided the table, He has sent men to call us, has received us when we came, and honored us with all other honor; but we, when we have offered insult to Him, to the company, and to the wedding, by our filthy garments, that is, our impure actions, are then with good cause cast out.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is to honor the marriage and the guests, that He drives off those bold and shameless persons; for were He to suffer those clothed in such a garment, He would seem to be offering insult to the rest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But may it never be that one, either of us or of other, find this of Him who has called us!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For to this end have all these things been written before they come to pass, that we, being sobered by the threats of the Scriptures, may not suffer this disgrace and punishment to go on to the deed, but stop it at the word only, and each with bright apparel come to that call; which may it come to pass that we all enjoy, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom and with whom, to the Father and the Holy Ghost, be glory for ever and ever.
